meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Skills Setup: Debugging for Students
content.seo_description: Learn to master Claude Code skills setup to conquer debugging hurdles. A beginner-friendly guide using Antigravity to accelerate your development journey.
content.answer_block: To overcome debugging paralysis, integrate Claude Code into your terminal to analyze stack traces in real-time. By pairing this with Antigravity’s context-aware prompting, you transform abstract errors into actionable code fixes, allowing you to learn the logic behind the bugs rather than just patching them blindly.
content.intro:
The screen flickers. A cryptic stack trace stares back at you, mocking your progress as you attempt to launch your first SaaS project. You’ve been staring at the same ten lines of code for hours, and the 'joy' of building has rapidly soured into the frustration of being stuck.

But this isn't a dead end—it's a rite of passage. Every senior engineer has stood exactly where you are. The difference between a frustrated student and a proficient builder is the toolset they use to bridge the gap between 'code that breaks' and 'code that ships.'

steps.title: Environment Initialization
steps.description: Configure your workspace to ensure Claude Code has read access to your project structure, enabling it to understand your file dependencies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project directory structure and identify potential dependency conflicts that could lead to runtime errors.

steps.title: Capturing the Trace
steps.description: Use Claude Code to ingest the raw error output from your terminal so it can map the failure to specific line numbers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Convert this raw stack trace into a simplified logical flow chart explaining exactly which module failed and why.

steps.title: Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Ask the AI to perform a 'blame' analysis, looking at recent changes that might have triggered the regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my last three commits and pinpoint the exact logic change that caused this specific variable to return null.

steps.title: Iterative Patching
steps.description: Implement a fix in a feature branch and have the AI verify the logic before you commit it to your main branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a robust refactor for this function that handles edge cases and prevents future null pointer exceptions.

steps.title: Validation and Testing
steps.description: Run automated tests to ensure the fix hasn't introduced new bugs in dependent modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a unit test suite covering the edge case we just fixed to ensure the code remains stable under load.

pro_tips: Always feed Claude the entire relevant file context, not just the function, to prevent hallucinated variable names.
Use Antigravity to document your debugging process; future-you will thank you when you encounter the same bug in six months.
Treat Claude Code as a pair-programmer, not a magic wand; ask it to explain the 'why' behind the fix to accelerate your learning.
Leverage the 'diff' feature to manually review every line of code Claude suggests before accepting it into your codebase.
Keep your terminal session clean; periodic restarts of Claude Code help it stay focused on the current task without 'memory fatigue'.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully patched your first bug, attempt to refactor your code to improve readability, then push your changes to GitHub to build your professional portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a developer learning to master the machine. Stop letting bugs stall your momentum and start shipping features that matter. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
